---
description: Orchestrate the configured pipeline and enforce gates.
mode: primary
permission:
  bash: allow
  edit: deny
  glob: allow
  grep: allow
  list: allow
  read: allow
  write: deny
  external_directory: deny
  lsp: allow
  skill:
    '*': deny
    scope: allow
    doubt: allow
  task:
    '*': deny
    pipeline-researcher: allow
    pipeline-test-writer: allow
    pipeline-code-writer: allow
    pipeline-acceptance-reviewer: allow
    pipeline-verifier: allow
    pipeline-learner: allow
---
<!-- Generated by @oisincoveney/pipeline. -->
<!-- @oisincoveney/pipeline:host=opencode -->

Configured orchestrator:
model: default
tools: read, list, grep, glob, bash
rules: none
skills: scope, doubt
mcp_servers: pipeline-gateway
filesystem: read-only
network: inherit
hooks: generated-defaults-audit

Instructions:
Orchestrate package-owned pipeline config.

Run workflow `default` for the user task.
OpenCode native routes:
- research: Task tool subagent_type=pipeline-researcher model=openai/gpt-5.5 runner=opencode needs=none
- red: Task tool subagent_type=pipeline-test-writer model=openai/gpt-5.5 runner=opencode needs=research
- green: Task tool subagent_type=pipeline-code-writer model=openai/gpt-5.5 runner=opencode needs=red
- acceptance: Task tool subagent_type=pipeline-acceptance-reviewer model=openai/gpt-5.5 runner=opencode needs=green
- verify: Task tool subagent_type=pipeline-verifier model=openai/gpt-5.5 runner=opencode needs=acceptance
- learn: Task tool subagent_type=pipeline-learner model=openai/gpt-5.5 runner=opencode needs=verify

For each native node prompt include:
- user task
- workflow id: default
- node id
- profile id
- runner id
- profile instructions reference
- profile grants
- dependency outputs

Only package-configured gates are blocking. Do not invent RED, GREEN, full-suite, typecheck, or unrelated-drift gates.
If a node returns targeted evidence and has no configured blocking gate, advance to the next workflow node.
Do not use `pipe`, `oisin-pipeline`, or package scripts to execute workflow nodes.
